source_text: 12. Text. Start the text on the second line below the subject line. The text will be single-spaced, left justified only. For guidance on plain English, slang, jargon, abbreviations, and acronyms, see chapter 12.
plain_language_rule: Start the body text on the second line below the subject line. Text must be single-spaced and left-justified only — do not use full or block justification. For writing guidance on plain English, slang, jargon, abbreviations, and acronyms, refer to Chapter 12 of the manual.
renderer_impact: Place body text starting on the second line below the subject line; use single line spacing; apply left justification only — no block, full, or right justification
notes: This section contains three deterministic layout rules (placement relative to subject line, single-spacing, left-only justification) and defers all writing-style guidance to Chapter 12. Plain English, slang, jargon, abbreviations, and acronym usage are referenced but not defined in this section — they are content-style rules, not layout rules. The validator should enforce only the deterministic layout rules; writing-style rules require separate Chapter 12 implementation. No paragraph-specific rules are included here (see C7-014 for paragraph formatting).
